_meta.investigation_scope: Routing audit of 19 skills — load position, sibling refs, agent mappings, when/do-not tables, conflicts, gaps

summary.count_mismatch_detail: descriptions-audit.json found 19 skills but cross-skill-conflict-audit listed only 15 valid names. Discrepancy: hivemind-architecture, hivemind-execution, hivemind-synthesis, and use-hivemind-ideating are not in the 15-skill registry but exist on disk.
undocumented_skills: context-intelligence (used as example in 6 skill-authoring reference files — SR-01)
verification-before-completion (referenced by multiple agents but not a standalone skill)

territory_overlaps.overlap: Refactor concept — TDD refactor is phase-scoped cleanup; hivemind-refactor is standalone structural refactoring
Requirement classification (Functional, Non-functional, Integration, Risk/Compliance, Operations) — identical five-bucket classification
territory_overlaps.resolution: Intentional consolidation — planning/SKILL.md line 271 explicitly states it consolidates hivemind-spec-driven

territory_overlaps.overlap: Investigation phase — codemap for structural mapping, debug for failure reproduction
territory_overlaps.resolution: Acceptable — different investigation modes with explicit handoff rules

critical_routing_issues.description: Routing table dispatches to 'use-hivemind-git-memory' for resume/trace/retrieve/anchor/enforce/index — but the skill IS use-hivemind-git-memory. It routes to itself. No sub-specialist skills exist.
general.md has 'write: true' and 'todowrite: allow' but description says 'Never mutates files'. Permission schema self-contradiction.
consolidates field lists its own name 5 times. Deprecation Notice also self-references 5 times.
Bundled Resources table references SKILL.md twice instead of listing distinct reference files
use-hivemind-delegation listed twice in Sibling Skills table (lines 264, 266). 'clean-code' at line 268 does NOT exist.
use-hivemind-tdd listed twice in Sibling Skills table (lines 175-176). 'review-and-refactor' does NOT exist — should be hivemind-refactor.

overlaps.rationale: TDD refactor is phase-scoped cleanup; hivemind-refactor is standalone structural refactoring — different scopes
Intentional consolidation — planning explicitly consolidates spec-driven
Different investigation modes (structural mapping vs failure reproduction) with explicit handoff rules
